tables.table_title: Model comparison on multi-horizon forecasting (normalized; lower is better)
tables.table_note: Gains over (i) pure Hawkes Attention suggest multi-frequency covariates matter; gains over (ii) pure wavelet approach suggest explicit event-time excitation helps when disruptions/irregularities are present.

tables.table_title: Anomaly detection via reconstruction (higher is better)
tables.table_note: Temporal context reduces redundant alerts, consistent with TRACE (Gadirov et al., 2026). Joint training with time-aware attention further improves separability.

tables.table_title: Representation geometric stability under perturbations (higher is better)
tables.table_note: Stability improves most under event dropping when Hawkes-style time modulation is present (Tan et al., 2026), and improves under noise when wavelet multi-frequency features are fused (Zhang et al., 2026). Level-guided fusion contributes consistent robustness (Kim et al., 2026; Raju, 2026 motivation).
